concerns.text: The plan assumes the existence of a-Si trajectories with pre-calculated thermal conductivity but admits 'no specific URL... has been pre-verified'. The pipeline requires paired data (structure + target) to test H-001/H-002. If the dataset lacks thermal conductivity metadata, the study cannot proceed without running Green-Kubo simulations, which the plan explicitly states may 'exceed the 6-hour CPU limit'. This creates a high risk of the project failing to produce results due to data modality mismatch (structure-only vs. structure+conductivity).

The plan assumes a dataset exists with 'pre-calculated' thermal conductivity to meet the 6-hour CPU limit. However, standard a-Si MD repositories (e.g., Zenodo, Materials Cloud) typically provide only atomic coordinates (xyz/lammps) and require the user to compute thermal conductivity via Green-Kubo or NEMD. If the selected dataset is structure-only, the 'download' phase will succeed, but the 'modeling' phase will fail due to missing targets, or the 'compute' phase will fail due to the 6-hour limit for Green-Kubo on large systems. The plan must identify a specific dataset that includes the target variable or explicitly budget for the simulation time.
